name: playwright-mcp
description: Official Microsoft Playwright MCP server. Wraps the Playwright browser-automation library as a Model Context Protocol server, so an AI agent can drive a real Chromium / Firefox / WebKit instance for testing, scraping, or visual inspection. We test the install on Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, and Windsurf.
gotchas: On first run, playwright-mcp downloads its browser bundles (Chromium / Firefox / WebKit). The download is ~300MB and only happens once, but the first tool call will hang for 30-90s while it completes. Plan for this in your client (Claude Desktop will show a 'tool running' spinner).

Headless mode is the default. If you want a visible browser, pass `--headed` via the args: `npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --headed`. Note: not all clients surface the headed-mode console, so headless is recommended for automation use cases.
The default viewport is 1280x720. Override with `--viewport-size 1920,1080` for retina screenshots or wider layouts.
On Linux servers without a display, you don't need to do anything special — Playwright bundles a virtual display. The `--no-sandbox` flag is needed only if you run the browser inside Docker.
notes: Universal install via npx is the canonical path because the package is published to npm as @playwright/mcp. Same mcpServers config string across all four clients we tested.
